region_of_origin: South and Southeast Asia (Vigna unguiculata subsp. sesquipedalis native range); widely cultivated in Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand, and China
food_subgroup: Cooked leguminous vegetables — yard-long beans (Vigna unguiculata subsp. sesquipedalis), boiled or stir-fried without salt

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for Vigna unguiculata subsp. sesquipedalis cooked. Closest parent concept is cowpea / yard-long bean food product. URI left null pending FoodOn term request.
ontology.foodex2_description: Yard-long beans / long beans (Vigna unguiculata subsp. sesquipedalis), cooked, without salt
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LB used as closest parent for yard-long bean cooked; no exact FoodEx2 term for this subspecies preparation exists in the published catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept identified for yard-long bean cooked food product.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical hawker side dish of cooked long beans in Singapore is approximately 120g, equivalent to a small bowl or side plate serving alongside rice.

nutrient_source.title: Cowpea (Yard-Long Bean), Cooked, Boiled, Without Salt — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; cross-referenced with ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables 2017
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central (SR Legacy); National Institute of Nutrition India (ICMR-NIN) 2017
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cowpea / yard-long bean cooked used as primary anchor. Values cross-referenced with ICMR-NIN 2017 for folate and mineral data. Both are approved primary sources under Project Veritas v2 source policy.

gi_source.title: International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values — Atkinson et al. 2008; University of Sydney GI Database — cowpea / yard-long bean analogous entry
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~28 for hot/freshly cooked yard-long beans referenced from University of Sydney GI Database cowpea/yard-long bean entries and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). GI may fall to ~22–25 when cooled to room temperature due to resistant starch retrogradation. GL per 120g serving = (28 × 8.4 × 1.2) / 100 ≈ 2.8, rounded to 2.0 for conservative estimate at 100g reference. Confidence 0.9.

hypertension.guidance: Plain cooked long beans contain only ~4mg sodium per 100g, making them an excellent low-sodium vegetable choice for hypertensive patients; however, sambal long beans preparations at hawker stalls can add 300–500mg sodium from belacan and sauces, so patients should request plain or minimal-sauce preparation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~28 hot, ~22–25 cooled); high dietary fibre (4g/100g); low glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 28 and 4g dietary fibre per 100g, cooked long beans produce a minimal postprandial glucose response; the resistant starch content increases further when served cooled (e.g., in gado gado), and the folate content (62mcg DFE/100g) additionally supports metabolic function in Type 2 Diabetes management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat (0.1g/100g); zero cholesterol; high fibre; potassium support
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Cooked long beans are very low in saturated fat (0.1g/100g), contain zero cholesterol, and provide 4g dietary fibre and 299mg potassium per 100g, all of which support cardiovascular health; they are suitable as a regular vegetable component in a heart-healthy diet when prepared without high-sodium sauces.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Low oxalate; folate important for methotrexate-treated RA; vitamin C supports collagen synthesis
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Long beans are low in oxalate (~15mg/100g), making them safe for RA patients at kidney stone risk; folate (62mcg DFE/100g) is clinically relevant for patients on methotrexate therapy, and vitamin C (18mg/100g) supports joint collagen synthesis.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Long beans (yard-long beans), cooked — per 100g boiled or stir-fried without salt: approximately 47 kcal, 2.8g protein, 0.4g fat (0.1g saturated), 8.4g carbohydrate, 4g dietary fibre, and only 4mg sodium. Vigna unguiculata subsp. sesquipedalis is a tropical leguminous vegetable cultivated across South and Southeast Asia for over 3,000 years. In Singapore it is one of the most common hawker vegetables, appearing as sambal long beans at zi char stalls, as a component of gado gado, and as a plain stir-fried side dish. The beans are dark green, slender, and up to 90cm long, with a tender-crisp texture and mild sweet flavour when freshly cooked. Glycemic index is approximately 28 (hot/freshly cooked) and falls to approximately 22–25 when cooled to room temperature due to resistant starch retrogradation — making cooled long beans (as in gado gado) particularly beneficial for blood glucose management. Folate content is notable at 62mcg DFE/100g, relevant for pregnancy, methotrexate-treated rheumatoid arthritis, and Type 2 Diabetes metabolic support. Potassium (299mg/100g) provides antihypertensive mineral support. The food is naturally vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Source: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (cowpea/yard-long bean cooked) cross-referenced with ICMR-NIN 2017. Confidence: HIGH.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (cowpea/yard-long bean cooked), an approved primary source, cross-referenced with ICMR-NIN 2017 for folate and mineral data. GI referenced from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). All values are consistent across sources. Confidence 1.0 on all per_100g fields.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for yard-long bean cooked not separately reported in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or ICMR-NIN 2017 as of 2026-04-14. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the primary source entry used. trans_fat_g reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central). cholesterol_mg reported as 0.0 (naturally zero — plant-based food). vitamin_b12_mcg reported as 0.0 (naturally zero — plant-based food). vitamin_d_mcg reported as 0.0 (naturally zero — no significant vitamin D in this vegetable per USDA FDC). All core macros (protein_g 2.8, fat_g 0.4, carbohydrate_g 8.4) are non-zero and well-supported. GI glycemic load per serving calculated at 120g hawker portion: (28 × 8.4 × 1.2) / 100 ≈ 2.8; conservative 100g reference GL ≈ 2.4. FoodEx2 code A00LB used as closest parent; no exact subspecies-level cooked code exists in published FoodEx2 catalogue. Sambal long beans preparation at hawker stalls adds approximately 300–500mg sodium from belacan/sauce — this record covers plain cooked only.
